title: Usage guidelines
description: When to use, when not to use, and do/don't pairs with concrete UI examples

sections.whenToUse: 2-4 bullet points describing scenarios where this component is the right choice. Focus on the user's task, not abstract descriptions. Each bullet should be a specific scenario.
sections.whenNotToUse: 2-4 bullet points. Each must name the ALTERNATIVE component to use instead (e.g., 'Multi-line content → use Textarea'). Never just say 'don't use for X' without naming the alternative.
sections.doDont: 1-3 pairs of do/don't examples. Each pair should show a concrete UI scenario, not an abstract guideline. doLabel and doDetail describe the correct approach, dontLabel and dontDetail describe the incorrect approach.

qualityRules: whenToUse bullets must describe specific user tasks, not component capabilities
whenNotToUse bullets must ALWAYS name the alternative component with → arrow syntax
doDont pairs must show concrete UI scenarios — labels visible on the component, not abstract principles
doDont doDetail and dontDetail must explain WHY, not just restate the label
Minimum 2 doDont pairs for components with rich interaction patterns
